task_name: Client — tool event types, collection, collapsible trace UI

concerns_addressed: Cycle-2 blocking fix #3 (tautological wiring test) is genuinely resolved: deriveToolTrace is now an EXPORTED pure function in BoardContext.tsx:45-61; the live-derivation useEffect (BoardContext.tsx:181-183) calls that SAME exported function via setToolTrace(deriveToolTrace(agentEvents)); ToolTrace.test.tsx:4 imports deriveToolTrace from ../context/BoardContext and drives it directly (ToolTrace.test.tsx:92,118,135,139), asserting the derived ToolTraceItem[] output. The logic is no longer re-implemented inline in the test — breaking/removing the production function breaks both the effect and the test together, so it would FAIL. This is the option-2 path explicitly sanctioned by the cycle-2 fix instruction.

No regression to previously-PASSED items: AgentEvent union extended with agent.tool.started/result/failed plus toolName/query/resultCount/errorCode/attempt (types.ts:206-226); ToolTraceItem shape present (types.ts:171-179). ToolTrace.tsx is collapsed-by-default (useState(false), line 10), expands on click (line 29), renders nothing on empty steps (line 12), shows failed 'error' badge + errorCode detail on errorCode (lines 22,35-39,66-70). Replay (R5): AgentPage.tsx:233-236 syncs stored board.toolTrace into BoardContext for ContextPanel via setToolTrace; ContextPanel.tsx:322-334 renders <ToolTrace steps={toolTrace}> as a section separate from the card output body (must-not satisfied). Camel design tokens used (neutral-/primary-/error- + lucide icons), no claude.ai styling. No localStorage/sessionStorage. No server-side changes in scope.

Non-blocking carried observation (does NOT break a deliverable): two uncoordinated toolTrace writers remain (BoardContext.tsx:182 derives from live agentEvents; AgentPage.tsx:235 writes stored board.toolTrace). Verified R5 replay ordering holds: on reopen with no live SSE, agentEvents is [] so the BoardContext effect writes deriveToolTrace([])=[] at mount, and AgentPage's stored-trace write lands after the async board fetch — final state is the stored trace, so replay works without re-running search. ContextPanel renders the single shared global trace regardless of selected card — also a carried observation, not a deliverable break.

strengths: Derivation extracted into a single exported pure function (deriveToolTrace) used by both production effect and test — eliminates duplication and makes the wiring genuinely testable (DRY, single source of truth).
Test coverage strengthened beyond the minimum: added cases for agent.tool.failed inclusion, no-tool-events filtering, and empty input (ToolTrace.test.tsx:107-140), all driving the real function.
All 8 ToolTrace tests pass; full client suite 78/78 passes with no regressions.
Component cleanly uses Camel design tokens and lucide icons consistent with the creative brief; clear summary/detail separation with accessible role=button toggle and data-testid hook.

issues.description: Two uncoordinated writers to toolTrace state (BoardContext live derivation vs AgentPage stored-trace sync). Verified harmless for the current replay/live scenarios, but the implicit reliance on effect ordering is fragile and worth a follow-up consolidation.
ContextPanel renders the single global toolTrace for any opened card rather than scoping to the selected card/column; acceptable for the current single-board agent flow but not card-scoped.
